title: Copy-paste + class-balance ablation decision artifact
description: OUTPUT-ONLY schema for the final runs/_copy_paste_decision.json artifact (plan docs/planning/additional_components_plan.md §3.7). Sister-file precedent: components/hmm_smoother/gates/_hmm_decision_schema.json (locked iter-3 2026-05-09). C3 NEW-MAJOR 3+4 2026-05-09: headline_arm enum no longer accepts 'no_aug' (the baseline reference cell carries no decision); MetricsBlock split into BaselineMetricsBlock (is_baseline_reference=true + all-zero deltas) and CandidateMetricsBlock (is_baseline_reference=false) so a sweep cell can't silently masquerade as baseline. Validation library: `jsonschema` (Draft-7), matching scripts/_r2_decision_schema.json's precedent.

headline_arm.description: C3 NEW-MAJOR 3 2026-05-09: 'no_aug' is REJECTED at the schema boundary because the baseline reference cell carries no decision. AblationConfig.__post_init__ rejects 'no_aug' as anchor_arm at the runtime boundary; mirror that contract here so a malformed artifact (e.g. handwritten test fixture) fails schema validation rather than silently producing a meaningless top-level decision.

headline_beta.description: null when headline_arm == 'cp_only'; otherwise must match the cp_balanced.deploy_anchor_beta field.

no_aug.description: Baseline reference; no decision is computed (the rule is undefined on a self-comparison). All MetricsBlock delta fields are constrained to exactly 0 by BaselineMetricsBlock — the runner asserts this before serialization, and the schema mirrors at the boundary.

deploy_anchor_beta.description: Which β value populates the top-level headline_* fields. Must match exactly one sensitivity_sweep row's beta. Plan §3.7 sensitivity-sweep set is fixed; deviation requires re-running the §三 adversarial loop.

sensitivity_sweep.description: Plan §3.7 sweeps β ∈ {0.99, 0.999, 0.9999}. The allOf+contains tuple combined with minItems/maxItems=3 forces exactly one row per required β (Draft-7 has no minContains/maxContains, mirroring _hmm_decision_schema.json's pattern). Exactly one row MUST set deploy_anchor=true; anchor-uniqueness is a runner runtime invariant since JSON Schema cannot express 'exactly one of array elements has property X = true'.

map_no_regression.description: C3 iter-9 NEW-MAJOR 2026-05-09: upstream eval verdict for THIS arm vs baseline. Per-cell so the artifact carries the verdict that drove the decision. For BaselineMetricsBlock this is constrained to const true (self-comparison).

map_regression_tolerance_pp.description: C3 iter-9 NEW-MAJOR 2026-05-09: tolerance applied when computing map_no_regression. Cap at 0.5 mirrors DecisionInputs.DROP_TOTAL_MAP_REGRESSION_PP — beyond that the deploy guard tolerates regressions the drop trigger rejects.

data_yaml_sha256.description: C3 iter-9 NEW-MAJOR 2026-05-09: source data.yaml hash. Class-label provenance — runner enforces equality across all 5 evals AND between top-level data_yaml_sha256, config, counts, and every metrics block.

BaselineMetricsBlock.description: C3 NEW-MAJOR 4 2026-05-09: the no_aug arm's metrics block. is_baseline_reference is fixed true and every delta field is fixed 0 (the baseline is constructed by self-comparison; non-zero deltas indicate a runner construction bug). Without this constraint, a no_aug.metrics block could mark itself as candidate (is_baseline_reference=false) and the resulting artifact would be semantically broken even though all numeric ranges pass. C3 iter-9 NEW-MAJOR 2026-05-09: also pins map_no_regression: const true (the baseline self-comparison has no regression possible).

CandidateMetricsBlock.description: C3 NEW-MAJOR 4 2026-05-09: cp_only and cp_balanced sweep cells. is_baseline_reference is fixed false so a candidate cell can't silently masquerade as baseline.
